threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a Tube strike is just another inconvenience, not a monthly rite of passage that brings the city to a standstill.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with 90s R&B. You could feel the eye rolls turn into head nods before the next stop.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid in a raincoat riding a bicycle past the kebab shop while an old man fed pigeons—felt like script of our daily lives.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: On Sundays, the flea markets here turn into impromptu techno raves, complete with DJs spinning while you hunt for vintage vinyl. Only in Berlin!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they instinctively avoid the Civic Center BART station after 5 PM like it's a fire zone.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni stuck behind a street performer juggling flaming clubs at Civic Center. Only in SF would the system have to wait for a performance.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think of Gràcia as just a pretty center, but they miss the midday siesta culture. Most locals vanish for hours, and the real vibe is during the evenings.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their wide-eyed wonder at how late the bars serve dinner. Locals have perfected the art of the late-night tapa.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the Tube instead of the Overground. Bonus points if you’ve yet to learn that no one says 'the' Dalston.
Last week on the 35, sat next to a guy singing old reggae tracks loudly. He claimed it was a tribute to the 'real Brixton'—nobody argued.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a toddler shouting 'Ich will kein Gemüse!' from a stroller while their hipster parent scrolled on their phone at the market.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a dog park with a mini beer garden right next to it. Watch the pups while sipping a local brew—pure Berlin vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a local knows to avoid the BART escalator at Civic Center after 5 PM; it freezes like a block of ice. Once, I slipped and nearly went flying.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Waiting at Civic Center, a group of tourists asked a Muni driver for directions. He smiled and pointed them right back to the same bus they missed.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the fancy bars, but they miss the real vibe in its quiet corners where locals sip café amb llet on stoops.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by their confused faces while ordering a coffee; they still ask for milk in a cortado.
